chapter_title.en: Chapter 19 – Wiping the head and the two feet

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/105)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/105)
text: تَوَضَّأَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) فَغَسَلَ وَجْهَهُ وَ ذِرَاعَيْهِ ثُمَّ مَسَحَ عَلَى رَأْسِهِ وَ عَلَى نَعْلَيْهِ وَ لَمْ يُدْخِلْ يَدَهُ تَحْتَ الشِّرَاكِ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Washa, from Aban, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘Aliasws performed Ablution (Wudhu), so heasws washed hisasws face, and hisasws forearm. Then heasws wiped upon hisasws head, and upon hisasws slipper (open from the front), and did not enter hisasws hand beneath the straps’.
en.sarwar: 11. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Washsha’ from Aban from Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Ali, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, once took Wudu’. He washed his face and forearms, then wiped on top of his head and over his shoes but he did not push his hand under his shoelaces.’” (Fatwa best explains this Hadith.)
